hypothesis_en: Mr President, after 11 September this Parliament worked swiftly and very seriously, supporting the Commission and asking the Council for a series of measures to tackle terrorism. In addition, efforts were made to establish minimum procedural guarantees and, in short, to create a framework that could provide us with greater security. This was also the case during the debate on the new Constitution, which would open up an important window of opportunity to improve the legal space in which to establish police and judicial cooperation and to subject it to the much-needed parliamentary control. It has been necessary to live the horror that we have lived to come back to speak in a solemn session on a Wednesday morning about it. The European Commission is right when it says that we do not need new ideas, we just need to put into practice those that are already on the table, boring and too handled. We can and must make a difference. We do not need a European patriotic act. We can and must offer more security on the basis of mutual trust and strong cooperation, in strict respect for the freedoms and rights that terrorism wants to destroy. I would like to ask the Commissioner whether he is aware of the fact that the European Parliament has not yet been consulted on this matter, and if he is aware of the fact that the European Parliament has not yet been consulted on this matter.
reference_en: thank you President after the eleventh of September the Parliament worked very fast and very seriously supporting the Commission and asking for a series of measures to combat terrorism the institutions in the Union started to work effik= efficiently in order to obtain a common definition of the crime of terrorism so that we could (1.78) start t= to have the European arrest warrants and so on so we have had many statements now calling for more police cooperation and beefing up the chiefs of police task force making sure Europol could be more effective and strengthened (1.31) also we talked about efforts to establish minimum guarantees for trials (1.05) and basically to set up a framework which might give us greater safety greater agility given situations such as the one which happened in the US this happened in the course of the debate on the new Constitution which gave us a framework of important opportunity in order to enhance our legal area within Europe boosting police and judicial cooperation and also to ensure that there is parliamentary control of these things but we missed the moment and the measures suggested (1.86) were not pushed forward there was a= an inertia (1.45) and therefore the necessary steps have not been taken we have to accept that we made a mistake and we have to come back to have a proper solemn session such as this where we can discuss all these things Mr President on the eleventh of March all of us played a sad role (1.18) what the citizens expect from us is cooperation in order to create security now for various reasons (2.31) what (1.79) we had in the end was a ceremony of confusion the Commission is right when they say that we don't need new ideas we simply need to put into practice the ideas which we have on the table before us we already have the drafts and they have not been properly implemented (2.27) (1.4) we now have this new idea of having a Mr Terrorism (1.1) which will lead to nothing unless we improve the overall framework of mutual confidence and trust Europe must make a difference we don't need a European Patriot Act we have to offer greater security by confidence building and strong cooperation strictly respecting human rights the human rights and freedoms which terrorism seeks to destroy I hope that the next time we talk about this we won't have fresh tears on our eyes but we will come forward with a strong will to face up to today's world a world in which Europe (1.12) works on the basis of internal cooperation and can offer a guide a pathway within the framework of the UN and in the framework of multilateral actions in order to tackle the huge dangers which face us today thank you President
source_es: gracias señor Presidente después del once de septiembre este Parlamento trabajó ~defprisa y muy en serio apoyando a la Comisión y pidiendo al Consejo una serie de medidas para hacer frente al terrorismo las instituciones de la Unión se pusieron a trabajar con eficacia para obtener una definición común del delito de terrorismo para obtener y poner en marcha la orden de búsqueda y captura se hicieron en esta sede y en otras sedes comunitarias grandes proclamas a favor de una mayor cooperación policial a p= favor de reforzar la task force de jefes de policía a favor de un Europol operativo bajo control judicial efectivo se hicieron además esfuerzos para conseguir (1.03) el establecimiento de garantías mínimas procesales y en definitiva para crear un marco que pudiera ofrecernos mayor seguridad mayor agilidad ante situaciones como la que se había producido en Estados Unidos esto sucedía además durante el debate sobre la nueva Constitución que abría un marco de oportunidades importante para mejorar el espacio jurídico en el que encardinar la cooperación policial y judicial y para someterla al muy necesario control parlamentario pero el momento pasó y las medidas propuestas fueron avanzando sólo por inercia a un ritmo señor Presidente que aún hoy no han llegado a ninguna parte ha sido necesario vivir el horror que hemos vivido para que volvamos a hablar en sesión solemne un miércoles por la mañana de ello señor Presidente el once de marzo todos hicimos un triste papel si hay algo que los ciudadanos esperaban de nosotros es cooperación para ofrecer seguridad y lo que escenificamos por distintas razones y con distintos grados de responsabilidad fue una verdadera ceremonia de la ~con_fusión tiene razón la Comisión Europea cuando dice que no necesitamos nuevas ideas necesitamos sólo poner en práctica las que están sobre la mesa ya aburridas y demasiado manoseadas aún así no hemos escapado a la tentación de tener una nueva y hemos creado un Mister terrorismo que no sé muy bien qué hará si no mejoramos el marco global de confianza mutua Europa puede y debe marcar la diferencia no necesitamos una Patriot Act europea podemos y debemos ofrecer más seguridad a partir de confianza mutua y de cooperación fuerte en estricto respeto a las libertades y derechos que el terrorismo quiere destruir espero que la próxima vez que hablemos de esto no sea con lágrimas aún recientes sino con la voluntad firme de estar a la altura del mundo de hoy un mundo en el que Europa trabajando a favor de una cooperación interna puede ofrecer también un camino y una guía para en el marco de Naciones Unidas y en el marco de una acción multilateral hacer frente a los peligros importantes a los que nos enfrentamos gracias señor Presidente
